tasks.nl_intent: @home_hub, set the @living_room_thermostat to 21 degrees in heat mode.

hints.target_device: The device whose state is being changed — NOT @home_hub, which is the recipient executing the command.
hints.setpoint_celsius: The target temperature value in Celsius.
hints.mode: The thermostat operating mode: one of heat|cool|auto|off.

tasks.nl_intent: @fulfillment, ship order #ORD-4821 to customer @maria_chen via express shipping.

hints.order_id: The order being shipped — NOT @fulfillment (the recipient agent) and NOT the customer.
hints.ship_to: The customer who receives the parcel — NOT @fulfillment, which only performs the action.
hints.shipping_method: Delivery service tier: one of standard|express|overnight.

tasks.nl_intent: @automation_engine, create a rule: when @front_door_sensor detects motion, turn on @porch_light.

hints.trigger_device: The device whose event STARTS the rule (the cause) — NOT the device that gets actuated.
hints.trigger_event: The sensed event that fires the rule: one of motion_detected|door_opened|temperature_high.
hints.action_device: The device acted upon as a RESULT (the effect) — NOT the trigger sensor.
hints.action: What is done to the action_device: one of turn_on|turn_off|toggle.

tasks.nl_intent: @billing, refund $45.00 to @james_wong for order #ORD-7733 because the item arrived damaged.

hints.order_id: The order the refund is against — NOT @billing (the actor) and NOT the customer.
hints.refund_recipient: Who receives the money back — NOT @billing, which issues the refund.
hints.reason: Cause of the refund: one of damaged|wrong_item|not_delivered|customer_request.

tasks.nl_intent: @home_hub, have the @bedroom_speaker play playlist #chill-evening at volume 30.

hints.target_device: The speaker that plays the audio — NOT @home_hub, which relays the command.
hints.content_id: The playlist resource to play — a content reference, NOT the device.

tasks.nl_intent: @warehouse, for order #ORD-9001 pick items in this exact sequence — #SKU-A, then #SKU-B, then #SKU-C — and assign the job to picker @raj.

hints.order_id: The order being fulfilled — NOT @warehouse (recipient) and NOT the picker.
hints.pick_sequence: SKUs in the REQUIRED pick order; sequence is meaningful, do not reorder or dedupe.
hints.assignee: The picker who performs the job — NOT @warehouse, the recipient agent.

tasks.nl_intent: @home_hub, activate the scene named Movie Night, which affects @living_room_light, @blinds, and @kitchen_light.

hints.scene_name: The human-readable scene label as free text — NOT a device reference.
hints.affected_devices: The unordered SET of devices the scene controls; membership matters, order does not — NOT @home_hub.

tasks.nl_intent: @logistics, transfer order #ORD-5500 from warehouse @wh_north to warehouse @wh_south.

hints.order_id: The order being moved — NOT @logistics (the actor).
hints.source_warehouse: The ORIGIN the order leaves (from) — NOT the destination.
hints.dest_warehouse: The DESTINATION the order arrives at (to) — NOT the source.

tasks.nl_intent: @home_hub, if @smoke_detector_kitchen triggers, send a critical alert to @homeowner_phone — do not alert the hub itself.

hints.trigger_device: The device whose event raises the alert (the cause) — NOT the alert's recipient.
hints.alert_recipient: Where the alert is delivered — NOT @home_hub and NOT the triggering detector.
hints.severity: Alert urgency level: one of info|warning|critical.

tasks.nl_intent: @support_bot, escalate ticket #TKT-330 (for order #ORD-8120) to agent @supervisor_lin, mark it urgent, and note that customer @nina_patel is waiting.

hints.ticket_id: The support ticket being escalated — NOT the order it concerns.
hints.order_id: The order the ticket is about — NOT the ticket id and NOT the customer.
hints.escalate_to: The agent who receives the escalation — NOT @support_bot (the actor) and NOT the customer.
hints.customer: The end customer awaiting resolution — NOT the escalation target agent.
